title: Did Syracuse get all the IT work it paid millions for? Outside report calls for tighter oversight
summary: An $88,000 audit by Bonadio Group found no fraud in Syracuse's $3M EY payroll modernization contract but raised concerns that EY may not have completed all billed work. The report identified systemic IT governance weaknesses and recommended establishing a Chief Technology Officer position to improve oversight of decentralized software acquisition and project management.

political_signal.description: City Council commissioned independent audit following whistleblower complaint and public controversy. Report validates concerns about incomplete work delivery and systemic governance gaps, increasing pressure for CIO/CTO position and vendor accountability mechanisms.

processing_notes: Dollar amount $3M appears incomplete in source (shown as '$3'); interpreted as $3 million based on context
Article truncated; recommended analysis of remaining content for additional findings/recommendations
Key absence: Two senior officials (Caliva, May) declined interviews, limiting audit depth on procurement decisions
CTO budget rejection noted as prior obstacle to governance improvement
Whistleblower complaint substance (pass-through contract mechanism) not fully adjudicated by audit
